messages.content:
Which of the following reference sources will you find the rules pertaining to RPA operations?

Review Later
AIP ENR
CAR(1988) part 101
CASR (1998) part 101
AIP GEN
The rules pertaining to RPA (Remotely Piloted Aircraft) operations can be found in CASR (1998) part 101.

How does a multicopter achieve yaw control to the right ?

Review Later
By increasing rpm of 2 motor that turns CCW and reduce rpm of the 2 motor that turns CW
by pivoting all the motors
by using rudder
by rolling to the right

A multicopter achieves yaw control to the right by increasing the RPM (rotation per minute) of the two motors that turn counter-clockwise (CCW) and reducing the RPM of the two motors that turn clockwise (CW).

Where would be the ideal location of the GPS antenna in an RPA

Review Later
On the top of the body
Under the body
On the motor arms
Next to the video transmission module
The ideal location for the GPS antenna in an RPA (Remotely Piloted Aircraft) would be on the top of the body.

What is the formula to calculate an object’s moment for weight and balance purposes?

Review Later
W x A = M
I = V/R
W = P/T
F x D = E

The formula to calculate an object's moment for weight and balance purposes is:

Weight x Arm (distance) = Moment (W x A = M)

What is rotational airflow?

Review Later
Wingtip vortices
Airflow caused by the rotation of the rotors
Wake turbulence caused by other aircraft
The component of airflow due to wind and airspeed

Rotational airflow refers to the airflow caused by the rotation of the rotors in an aircraft. It is specifically related to the airflow generated by the spinning rotors of a helicopter or a multicopter.

What will help the RPA climb at a lower rotor RPM?

Review Later
Flying down wind into disturbed air
Flying upwind into undisturbed air
Increasing power
Decreasing power
Increasing power will help the RPA climb at a lower rotor RPM.

What will help the RPA climb at a lower rotor RPM?

Review Later
Flying down wind into disturbed air
Flying upwind into undisturbed air
Increasing power
Decreasing power
To help the RPA climb at a lower rotor RPM, flying upwind into undisturbed air would be more effective.

What effects can overweight cause to the RPA in vertical flight mode?

Review Later
More power required to hover
Increased wake turbulence
More susceptible to Vortex Ring State
All the above

All of the above effects can be caused by overweight in the RPA during vertical flight mode. An overweight RPA will require more power to hover, experience increased wake turbulence, and become more susceptible to the Vortex Ring State phenomenon.

How does a multirotor ascend/climb

Review Later
By increasing the RPM of the front side motors
By increasing the RPM of the all motors
By using elevator
by flying faster
A multirotor ascends or climbs by increasing the RPM of all motors. Increasing the RPM of all the motors generates more lift, allowing the multirotor to ascend or climb vertically.

During flight, which stick on a Mode 2 transmitter operates the lateral (sideways) control?

Review Later
Right Stick forward and back
Left stick forward and back
Right stick left and right
Left stick left and right
On a Mode 2 transmitter, the left stick operated by moving it left and right provides lateral (sideways) control during flight.

Which of the following is more correct as the result of a motor failing during the flight of a standard 4 rotor Multirotor RPA (ie DJI Phantom or Mavic series)?

Review Later
Instant crash
No change
Attempt to glide to land
Transition back to VTOL mode and prepare to land

The more correct option as a result of a motor failing during the flight of a standard 4 rotor multirotor RPA (such as DJI Phantom or Mavic series) would be "Attempt to glide to land." In such a situation, the drone would attempt to maintain stability and use the remaining functional motors to glide back and safely land.

The clockwise and anticlockwise rotor blades can be installed in any order on a multirotor. True or false?

Review Later
True
False

False. The clockwise and anticlockwise rotor blades on a multirotor should be installed in a specific order. Typically, the clockwise (CW) rotor blades are installed in a certain order and the anticlockwise (CCW) rotor blades in another specific order. This ensures correct rotation and proper balance during operation.

How should a Multirotor be launched and retrieved?

Review Later
Upwind
Crosswind
Downwind
Multirotors launch and land vertically, so wind direction is not overly relevant.

Multirotors should generally be launched and retrieved vertically, so the wind direction is not overly relevant. However, if there are significant winds, it is best to consider launching and retrieving the multirotor upwind to ensure better control and stability during these operations.

What effect will an increase of aircraft load weight have on climb rates?

Review Later
Nothing
Climb rates will improve
More thrust will be required to maintain the climb rate
Less thrust will be required to maintain the climb rate

An increase in aircraft load weight will typically result in climb rates being negatively affected. More thrust will be required to maintain the same climb rate, meaning that the aircraft will need to work harder to ascend. Therefore, the statement "More thrust will be required to maintain the climb rate" is correct.

What affect will higher temperature have on flying?

Review Later
Atmospheric air density decreases as temperature rises, generating less lift and thrust
The pilot will require more water
Radio signal range will decrease
Nothing, temperature has no effect on flying

Atmospheric air density decreases as temperature rises, leading to a decrease in lift and thrust generation. Therefore, higher temperature can affect flying by reducing the performance capabilities of the aircraft. The statement "Atmospheric air density decreases as temperature rises, generating less lift and thrust" is accurate in this context.

Why would we want to perform a flat turn ?

Review Later
Change direction
Pan with a fixed camera
Position the craft for the next part of the mission
Any of the above

Performing a flat turn can serve various purposes, including changing direction, panning with a fixed camera, or positioning the craft for the next part of the mission. Therefore, the statement "Any of the above" is correct. The specific reason for performing a flat turn will depend on the specific situation and objectives of the flight.

The heavier the multirotor the greater the risk of entering vortex ring state, True or false?

Review Later
True
False

False. The statement is incorrect. The risk of entering the vortex ring state (VRS) is typically greater for lighter multirotors rather than heavier ones. VRS occurs when a descending rotor's downwash interferes with the inflow of air into the rotor system. It is more likely to happen at low forward speeds and high rates of descent. Therefore, lighter multirotors are more susceptible to VRS because they are more maneuverable and can descend faster.

It is okay to approach a large multirotor while it is flying just before touching the ground?

Review Later
Yes as being close to spinning blades is safe
No, as there is a possibility for a failure before landing
Yes, the pilot is in command 100% of the time
Yes, Correct airspeed is required to balance drag, lift and maximum range
Maybe if it looks safe

No, it is not okay to approach a large multirotor while it is flying just before touching the ground. There is always a possibility of a failure during landing, which could pose a safety risk to those nearby. It is important to maintain a safe distance from the aircraft during landing and follow appropriate safety protocols.

What is vertical distance in aviation measured in?

Review Later
Metres
Yards
Nautical Miles
Feet
Vertical distance in aviation is typically measured in feet.

Which of the following is a true statement?

Review Later
A UAV flying has a higher potential energy than one on the ground
A UAV on the ground has a higher potential energy than one in the air.
A UAV on the ground and in the air have the same potential energy
None of the above
A UAV flying has a higher potential energy than one on the ground.

The cross section of a propeller resembles what?

Review Later
A wing cross section
A circle
A flat surface
A wheel
The cross section of a propeller typically resembles a wing cross section.

Turning the Yoke to the right or moving the control stick to the right on a fixed wing aircraft has what effect on the aircraft?

Review Later
Rolls to the left
Rolls to the right
Nose pitches up
Nose pitches down
Turning the yoke to the right or moving the control stick to the right on a fixed-wing aircraft has the effect of rolling the aircraft to the right.

Class C airspace is representative of which of the below:

Review Later
Major Airports
Regional Airports
Medium altitude airspace
High altitude airspace
Class C airspace is representative of major airports.

What information is contained in the ERSA?

Review Later
Weather and Terrain
Licensed and registered aerodromes, radio frequencies, radio navigation aids, runway and taxiway information
Prohibited, restricted and danger areas, including emergency procedures
Licensed and registered aerodromes, radio frequencies, radio navigation aids, runway and taxiway information and prohibited, restricted and danger areas, including emergency procedures

The ERSA (En Route Supplement Australia) contains licensed and registered aerodromes, radio frequencies, radio navigation aids, runway and taxiway information, as well as prohibited, restricted, and danger areas, including emergency procedures.

Where can you find information on interpreting meteorological information

Review Later
NOTAMs
AIP
ERSA
Channel 7 News
Information on interpreting meteorological information can be found in the AIP (Aeronautical Information Publication).

The time stated on a NOTAM is?

Review Later
Local Time
UTC
UTM
Daylight savings time
The time stated on a NOTAM (Notice to Airmen) is typically in UTC (Coordinated Universal Time).

At least how many hours' notice must CASA have for the lodgment of a NOTAM

Review Later
12hrs
24hrs
48hrs
72hrs
CASA (Civil Aviation Safety Authority) must be given at least 24 hours' notice for the lodgment of a NOTAM (Notice to Airmen).

A Topographical map represents what features?

Review Later
Vegetation Index by hypsometric tinting
Roads & locations
Elevation scale by hypsometric tinting
Minimum flying heights

A topographical map represents features such as elevation scale by hypsometric tinting, which indicates the varying heights of the land. It also includes information about roads and locations, but it typically does not display vegetation index or minimum flying heights.

Which is true of a dust devil?

Review Later
It is usually as intense as a tornado
It is typically poorly formed
It is a swirling updraft
It cannot happen on a sunny day
All the above

Among the options provided, the statement "It is a swirling updraft" is true of a dust devil. Dust devils are typically swirling columns of air that are caused by localized heating and rapid surface heating. However, they are usually much less intense than tornadoes and may vary in their form and occurrence, including on sunny days. Therefore, the correct option is "It is a swirling updraft."

In a Terminal Aerodrome Forecast we find a statement of the expected conditions at an airport during a period that is usually?

Review Later
1 hour
2 hours
10 hours
24 hours
None of the above
In a Terminal Aerodrome Forecast (TAF), the statement of expected conditions at an airport is typically provided for a period of 24 hours.

Battery electrical circuit
Battery elimination circuit
Brushless electrical circuit
Battery emergency circuit

Of the given options, "Battery emergency circuit" is the closest match. It refers to a circuit designed to handle emergency situations associated with the aircraft's battery, such as providing backup power or initiating emergency shutdown procedures. However, it is important to note that the exact terminology and circuitry may vary depending on the specific aircraft and its electrical system design.

Propeller size is expressed as a means of what dimensions

Review Later
Diameter x Thickness
Pitch x Hole diameter
Degrees of twist x RPM
Diameter x Pitch
Propeller size is typically expressed as a combination of diameter and pitch. Therefore, the correct answer is "Diameter x Pitch."

You measure the voltage of 2 Lithium Polymer battery packs one reads 3.7V and the other reads 7.4V what does this mean with the configuration of the battery.

Review Later
Battery is over charged
Second battery pack has an additional battery cell
Faulty voltmeter
Polarity was incorrect

The configuration of the battery packs can be determined based on the voltage readings. In this case, the battery pack reading 3.7V indicates a single-cell lithium polymer battery, while the battery pack reading 7.4V suggests a two-cell lithium polymer battery. Each cell of a lithium polymer battery typically has a nominal voltage of 3.7V. Therefore, the correct answer is "Second battery pack has an additional battery cell."

What does FHSS protocol with respect to radio transmission stand for?

Review Later
Fast Hopping Spread Spectrum
Frequency Hopping Spread Spectrum
Frequency Hosting Spread Spectrum
Frequency Hopping Speed

FHSS protocol, with respect to radio transmission, stands for "Frequency Hopping Spread Spectrum." This protocol involves rapidly hopping between different frequencies within a given band to enhance signal quality, reduce interference, and enhance security in wireless communication.

The global positioning system is built up around which fundamental components?

Review Later
Master Control Station
Monitor Station
Ground Stations
All the above

The global positioning system (GPS) is built up around all the above fundamental components, which include the Master Control Station, Monitor Station, and Ground Stations. These components work together to facilitate the functioning and accuracy of the GPS system.

Does the perception of colour improve with age?

Review Later
Colour improves with age
There is no change in colour perception over time
Colour perception will decrease with age
. Colour perception will only vary with your genetics

Colour perception tends to decrease with age. As individuals get older, their ability to perceive and differentiate certain colors may decline. This can be attributed to various factors, including changes in the eyes, lens yellowing, and a decrease in the number of color-sensitive cells in the retina. While there can be individual variations based on genetics, it is generally true that colour perception tends to decline with age.

How far should bystanders be from the launch/landing area?

Review Later
10 metres
15 metres
30 metres
Behind the pilot

Bystanders should typically be at a distance of at least 30 meters from the launch/landing area. This helps ensure their safety and minimizes the risk of any accidents or incidents involving the RPA (Remotely Piloted Aircraft).

At what level of Risk Rating should we try to lower the risk?

Review Later
We should try to lower the risk no matter what the level
Above 3
Above 6
When it hits 10

We should try to lower the risk no matter what the level. It is important to actively mitigate and reduce risks in any situation, regardless of the initial risk rating. Safety should always be a top priority, and proactive measures should be taken to minimize risks and ensure the well-being of individuals involved.

The RPA that you are operating has lost control link resulting in a runaway towards a non-controlled aerodrome can be classified as ?

Review Later
Minor Incident, no "separation in controlled airspace" breach, no one injured, no damage recorded - no report required.
Incident, need to be reported as soon as practicable
Serious incident, need to be reported immediately
Incident, need to be reported within 48 hrs

The situation described, where an RPA has lost control link and is heading towards a non-controlled aerodrome, can be classified as a serious incident. It would require immediate reporting to the appropriate authorities.

In the event of motor failure in the RPA that has an ability to resume flight after one motor failed

Review Later
Resume mission as its safe to do so
Land as soon as possible as it may lead to further failure
Activate parachute
Climb out to perform test

In the event of motor failure in an RPA that has the ability to resume flight after one motor has failed, it is generally recommended to land as soon as possible. Continuing the flight with a failed motor can potentially lead to further failures or compromise the safety and stability of the aircraft.

The process to obtain permission, approval and exemption at the approach and departure path will need the following

Review Later
Apply through the form 101-9 for approval
Operator holds an RePL
Operator holds or work under ReOC
All of the above

All of the above are required to obtain permission, approval, and exemption for operating in the approach and departure path. This includes applying through the appropriate form (such as form 101-9) for approval, holding a Remote Pilot License (RePL), and holding or working under a Remote Operator Certificate (ReOC).

an aerodrome in class C airspace and a clearance is required to operate within the boundary of that aerodrome
an aerodrome in class G airspace and doesn’t require a clearance to operate in but does require the use of a radio
an aerodrome in class G airspace and doesn’t require a clearance to operate and therefore a radio is not required for communication
an aerodrome in class C airspace and a clearance is not required to operate within the boundary of that aerodrome

The correct statement is: "An aerodrome in class C airspace requires a clearance to operate within its boundary." In class C airspace, operating within the boundary of the aerodrome typically requires obtaining clearance from
